meta.title: I Tested Every GPU
meta.description:
➡️ Get access to the ADVANCED-fine-tuning Repo (incl. future additions): https://trelis.com/ADVANCED-fine-tuning/
➡️ Runpod Affiliate Link: https://runpod.io?ref=jmfkcdio
➡️ One-click LLM Templates: https://github.com/TrelisResearch/one-click-llms
➡️ Thumbnail made with this tutorial: https://youtu.be/ThKYjTdkyP8

OTHER TRELIS LINKS:
➡️ Explore Developer Tools/Scripts: https://Trelis.com/
➡️ Trelis Newsletter: https://trelis.substack.com
➡️ Collaborate with Trelis: https://Trelis.com/developer-collaborations
➡️ Consulting: https://Trelis.com/

VIDEO LINKS:
- Slides: https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1UBuJvhTZKUTgP1Z-volhsG-monovjDNiyo6iXfc5-Ms/edit?usp=sharing
- Original llmperf repo: https://github.com/ray-project/llmperf
- Trelis' forked llmperf repo: https://github.com/TrelisResearch/llmperf
- sglang repo: https://github.com/sgl-project/sglang
- vLLM docs: https://docs.vllm.ai/
- llm compressor repo: https://github.com/vllm-project/llm-compressor

TIMESTAMPS:
00:00 - Introduction to GPU benchmarking test
00:36 - Test methodology and parameters
01:25 - Results: Time to first token
02:43 - Results: Token generation throughput
03:46 - Results: Price per million tokens
04:59 - Analysis of results ordering and trends
06:09 - Detailed discussion topics overview
07:00 - Why FP8 format is beneficial
08:19 - Technical explanation of FP8
10:45 - Comparison of different floating point formats
12:04 - GPU architecture support for FP8
14:21 - How FP8 works on different GPU architectures
17:13 - Live demonstration with RTX 3090
24:34 - SGLang vs vLLM comparison
32:17 - Caveats and limitations of benchmarking
37:34 - How to create FP8 models
39:09 - Using LLM Compressor for quantization
42:29 - Evaluating quantized models
45:48 - Conclusion and resources
